page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r8a.32xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server instance powered by the AMD EPYC 9T24 processor. Running on the KVM hypervisor, this instance features 128 dedicated physical cores with a single thread per core, operating at a clock speed of 2.7 GHz. It is equipped with 1024.0 GB of system memory, providing a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. The instance does not include local storage or GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage options.

Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 40 Gbps. The dedicated CPU allocation model ensures that each of the 128 cores operates without resource contention from other tenants, which is critical for predictable execution times. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the high density of memory relative to the core count positions this instance as a resource-dense option for workloads requiring large in-memory datasets without the overhead of maintaining multiple smaller nodes.

The hardware profile of the ecs.r8a.32xlarge makes it suitable for memory-intensive enterprise applications. Appropriate workloads include large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise resource planning systems, big data analytics pipelines, and memory-bound caching layers.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r8a.32xlarge is an x86_64 memory-optimized server instance powered by a 2.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9T24 processor. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, it features 128 dedicated physical cores with a single thread per core and 1024.0 GB of system memory, yielding an 8.0 GB per core ratio. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, but it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 40 Gbps. This dedicated resource allocation model ensures predictable performance for enterprise workloads. The high memory density and dedicated CPU architecture make this instance suitable for large-scale in-memory databases, big data analytics, and memory-intensive enterprise applications.

tagline: A dedicated x86_64 server featuring one terabyte of memory and one hundred twenty-eight physical cores for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
